title: Copper Recycling Startups Attract $2bn in VC Funding as Critical Metal Scarcity Bites
description: Copper Recycling Startups Attract $2bn in VC Funding as Critical Metal Scarcity Bites TechCrunch
htmlContent:
Copper Recycling Startups Attract $2bn in VC Funding as Critical Metal Scarcity Bites

By Maria Hernandez, TechCrunch • June 7, 2026

SAN FRANCISCO – A surge of venture capital has poured into copper recycling and urban mining startups in 2026, with more than $2 billion raised across the sector in the first half of the year alone, as investors position themselves to profit from the growing gap between the metal's supply and the insatiable demand created by the artificial intelligence infrastructure boom.

The largest deal of the year so far was a $450 million Series C round raised by Voltaic Metals, a San Jose-based startup that uses proprietary hydrometallurgical processes to recover high-purity copper from end-of-life electronics and industrial scrap. The round was led by Andreessen Horowitz's climate tech fund, with participation from Bill Gates' Breakthrough Energy Ventures and Alphabet's growth fund.

"Every data centre being built today will be a copper mine in 20 years," said Voltaic Metals CEO Priya Anand. "We are building the infrastructure to recover that copper efficiently, and the economics have never been better."

Secondary copper — produced from scrap rather than mined ore — already accounts for roughly 35% of global copper supply, but industry analysts say the percentage must rise substantially to fill the gap between primary mine supply and booming demand. Recycling also carries a significantly lower carbon footprint than primary smelting, making it attractive to technology companies with aggressive sustainability commitments.

Other notable fundraises in 2026 include a $320 million round for UK-based Circuit Metals, which focuses on recovering copper from printed circuit boards, and a $275 million raise by Australian firm RedLoop, which targets copper recovery from mining tailings using bio-leaching bacteria.

The venture capital interest marks a notable shift. For most of the past decade, copper recycling was considered a low-margin, capital-light business dominated by established industrial conglomerates. The combination of elevated copper prices, tightening supply, and the narrative of the AI supercycle has changed that calculus dramatically.

"When copper is at $10,000-plus per tonne and the demand story is as strong as it is right now, the economics of every tonne of secondary recovery look completely different from three years ago," said Lars Hoffman, partner at climate-focused VC firm Galvanize Capital. "This sector is having its solar moment."

Tags: copper, recycling, urban mining, venture capital, AI, startups, Voltaic Metals, Circuit Metals, sustainability

Sentiment signal: bullish
